metadata.title: Why the Rap Mortality Graph Lies
metadata.notes: Calling Bullshit (Vox Style), film two. Native vox plan. Color law: crimson = the censor window, navy = the data, golden highlighter = the rap bar (once). Chart bars in A03/A04 are a STYLIZED RECREATION of the described graph — narration commits only to 'past sixty' vs 'about thirty — half the others' (matches transcript); exact per-genre values are representative, flagged in FACTCHECK. The 'deadlier than war' line failed verification against the transcript and was cut at plan time. A13 fairness beat honors the study author's own caveat, per the book. Outro law pads A14 after audio.
_variant_todo: rewrite every beat narration_text in the Teardown register (voices/teardown/VOICE.md + CLAUDE-BRAND.md) — voice only, facts unchanged
signature tangent 0-1 per video, ONLY on a clear opportunity (see SKILL)
swap the outro to the Claude outro from AUTHOR.MD :: NikBearBrown
then build audience-namespaced (audio in the new voice, scenes in the new palette)

skin_warnings: NO RENDERABLE BEATS: no beat carries a shot.remotion.pattern or Manim annotation — the pipeline will render NOTHING and every slot becomes a YOU-slate. The sheet was authored without the scene contract.
A01: palette=claude but the cold open is 'un-annotated' — COLD OPEN LAW wants ClaudeComposerAsk
A14: palette=claude but the outro is 'un-annotated' — OUTRO LAW wants ClaudeTitleOutro

beats.narration_text: This is Liam, in for Bear. A few years ago, a chart went viral: jazz musicians live past sixty; rap musicians die at thirty. The data was real. The conclusion was garbage.
beats.tts_normalized_text: A few years ago, a chart went viral: jazz musicians live past sixty; rap musicians die at thirty. The data was real. The conclusion was garbage.

shot.note: cold open — boombox-and-microphone still life, flat editorial light; set shot.focus on the boombox grille

build.suggested: suggested prompt: [PHOTO] the era, as an object

beats.narration_text: This is the story of how honest numbers tell a lie — and of the one question that breaks the spell.
beats.tts_normalized_text: This is the story of how honest numbers tell a lie — and of the one question that breaks the spell.

card.title: THE RAP MORTALITY GRAPH
card.sub: a lie the data told itself

build.suggested: suggested prompt: This is the story of how honest numbers tell a lie — and of the one question that breaks the spell.

beats.narration_text: Here's the chart: average age at death, by genre. Jazz, blues, country, gospel: up past sixty. Rap and hip-hop: about thirty — half the others.
beats.tts_normalized_text: Here's the chart: average age at death, by genre. Jazz, blues, country, gospel: up past sixty. Rap and hip-hop: about thirty — half the others.

viz.note: genre bars draw up in navy; representative values (jazz 60 · blues 63 · country 62 · gospel 65 · rap 30 · hip-hop 29) — stylized recreation, FACTCHECK row A03; spoken commitment is only sixty-vs-thirty

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Here's the chart: average age at death, by genre. Jazz, blues, country, gospel: up past sixty. Rap and hip-hop: about thirty — half the others.

beats.narration_text: The gaps are huge, the pattern is clean, and the story writes itself: rap kills its stars.
beats.tts_normalized_text: The gaps are huge, the pattern is clean, and the story writes itself: rap kills its stars.

viz.note: golden highlighter sweeps the rap/hip-hop bars — the editor's pen, once

build.suggested: suggested prompt: The gaps are huge, the pattern is clean, and the story writes itself: rap kills its stars.

beats.narration_text: But hold on. Ask the question the chart is hoping you won't: who can even be in a dataset of dead musicians?
beats.tts_normalized_text: But hold on. Ask the question the chart is hoping you won't: who can even be in a dataset of dead musicians?

card.title: WHO CAN BE IN A DEAD-MUSICIANS DATASET?

build.suggested: suggested prompt: But hold on. Ask the question the chart is hoping you won't: who can even be in a dataset of dead musicians?

beats.narration_text: But the genres aren't the same age. Jazz, blues, country, gospel: a century old or more. Rap and hip-hop: forty years, at most.
beats.tts_normalized_text: But the genres aren't the same age. Jazz, blues, country, gospel: a century old or more. Rap and hip-hop: forty years, at most.

viz.note: horizontal genre-age timeline: century-long ink bars vs rap's short bar; 'century or more' and '40 years at most' are the transcript's own figures

build.suggested: suggested prompt: But the genres aren't the same age. Jazz, blues, country, gospel: a century old or more. Rap and hip-hop: forty years, at most.

beats.narration_text: A rapper who started at twenty in the eighties is in his fifties right now — alive, and therefore invisible to this chart.
beats.tts_normalized_text: A rapper who started at twenty in the eighties is in his fifties right now — alive, and therefore invisible to this chart.

shot.note: generic man in his fifties at a mixing desk, present day, no famous likeness, disclosed; shot.focus on his hands on the faders

beats.new_visual_element: [PHOTO] the survivor the chart cannot see

build.suggested: suggested prompt: [PHOTO] the survivor the chart cannot see

beats.narration_text: Living musicians can't appear in a dead-musicians dataset. So for a young genre, the only members who qualify are the ones who died young.
beats.tts_normalized_text: Living musicians can't appear in a dead-musicians dataset. So for a young genre, the only members who qualify are the ones who died young.

viz.note: isotype rows: living squares (navy) fade out of the young genre's row; only early-death squares (ink) remain

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Living musicians can't appear in a dead-musicians dataset. So for a young genre, the only members who qualify are the ones who died young.

beats.narration_text: The study window slams shut at the present day, and every lifespan still running simply vanishes from the data.
beats.tts_normalized_text: The study window slams shut at the present day, and every lifespan still running simply vanishes from the data.

viz.note: THE beat — lifespan bars along a timeline; a crimson vertical line slams down at 'today'; every bar crossing it vanishes (the chameleon figure's geometry, applied to musicians)

build.suggested: suggested prompt: The study window slams shut at the present day, and every lifespan still running simply vanishes from the data.

beats.narration_text: The trick has a name: right-censoring. The chart didn't measure how long rappers live — it measured how long rap has existed.
beats.tts_normalized_text: The trick has a name: right-censoring. The chart didn't measure how long rappers live — it measured how long rap has existed.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: The trick has a name: right-censoring. The chart didn't measure how long rappers live — it measured how long rap has existed.

beats.narration_text: Jazz has had a century to accumulate deaths at every age. Rap has had four decades — populated mostly by survivors.
beats.tts_normalized_text: Jazz has had a century to accumulate deaths at every age. Rap has had four decades — populated mostly by survivors.

viz.note: two isotype fields: jazz's century fills with deaths at all ages (ink squares across the range); rap's four decades — mostly navy (alive) squares

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Jazz has had a century to accumulate deaths at every age. Rap has had four decades — populated mostly by survivors.

beats.narration_text: Keep every lifespan identical and just chop the window: the doomed-looking gap rebuilds itself. Most of the viral pattern is exactly that.
beats.tts_normalized_text: Keep every lifespan identical and just chop the window: the doomed-looking gap rebuilds itself. Most of the viral pattern is exactly that.

viz.note: null model: identical lifespan bars per cohort; the crimson window chops; derived averages fall for young cohorts — 'most of the pattern' phrasing matches the book's own hedge ('we suspect the majority')

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Keep every lifespan identical and just chop the window: the doomed-looking gap rebuilds itself. Most of the viral pattern is exactly that.

beats.narration_text: To be fair, the study's author acknowledged the problem in her article. But charts travel alone — no caveat survives the screenshot.
beats.tts_normalized_text: To be fair, the study's author acknowledged the problem in her article. But charts travel alone — no caveat survives the screenshot.

card.title: SHE FLAGGED IT
card.sub: but no caveat survives a screenshot

build.suggested: suggested prompt: To be fair, the study's author acknowledged the problem in her article. But charts travel alone — no caveat survives the screenshot.

beats.narration_text: So when a chart says the young die young, first ask how old the category is — startups, new drugs, careers, all of it.
beats.tts_normalized_text: So when a chart says the young die young, first ask how old the category is — startups, new drugs, careers, all of it.

card.title: ASK HOW OLD THE CATEGORY IS

build.suggested: suggested prompt: So when a chart says the young die young, first ask how old the category is — startups, new drugs, careers, all of it.
